message:
search-core: add cass-compatible lexical query stack and optimize vector/refine paths

This commit consolidates low-level retrieval engine changes across lexical,
vector-index, and fusion layers. The goal is to improve cass compatibility,
reduce avoidable scoring overhead, and make full-recall paths behave better on
large candidate sets.

Lexical (cass_compat) enhancements:
- significantly expands `cass_compat` into a full query-construction surface
  instead of just schema/index lifecycle helpers
- adds tokenizer-aligned query sanitation and boolean tokenization supporting:
  - explicit AND/OR/NOT
  - quoted phrase terms
  - wildcard-aware terms with pattern classification
- adds wildcard translation logic:
  - exact/prefix term queries
  - suffix/substring/complex wildcard regex patterns
- adds bounded global regex query cache (`(field, pattern)` keyed) to avoid
  recompiling identical regex queries across interactive sessions
- adds structured filter application in Tantivy query construction for:
  - agent filters
  - workspace filters
  - created_at ranges
  - source-kind/source-id filters
- adds `cass_open_search_reader` helper to centralize reader open/reload policy,
  tokenizer registration, and schema field extraction
- adds/extends tests covering sanitizer behavior, empty-query all-query fallback,
  and filtered query construction
- updates exported API surface in `lexical::lib` to expose the new cass query
  builder/filter/token types and helper functions

Vector index search path improvements:
- introduces a fast collect+sort full-recall path when `k` is effectively
  unbounded (`k >= total_candidates`) and no filter is applied
- adds explicit all-candidate scanners for main index and WAL entries, including
  parallel collect-all chunking for main index scans
- factors a shared sorted-entry resolver to avoid duplicate resolve logic
- reduces heap churn in top-k scans by tracking dynamic cutoff and skipping
  insert attempts for clearly non-competitive scores
- exposes `score_key` as `pub(crate)` to reuse normalization logic in new
  cutoff checks
- adds regression tests proving collect-all ordering is consistent with heap
  top-k prefix behavior for both main-only and main+WAL scenarios

Fusion/two-tier adjustments:
- avoids repeated doc_id table lookups during phase-2 quality scoring by carrying
  `(index, doc_id)` pairs through the quality-scoring pipeline
- guards rerank invocation with an explicit non-empty rerank budget to avoid
  unnecessary work on empty result sets
- removes an unnecessary mutable binding in ANN branch (`two_tier`) cleanup

Minor lint hygiene:
- marks the no-op non-bundled model materializer as `const fn` in embed
  auto-detect to satisfy strict clippy constraints in this workspace.

Why grouped this way:
- all changes directly affect retrieval core behavior and query/scoring
  mechanics, independent of fsfs UI/transport concerns handled in the next
  commit.
